instruction: Send an email to a public park office asking about picnic reservations. The email must include the adjective "available" in the sense of "able to be used or obtained" and the verb "confirm" in the sense of "establish the truth or correctness of something." Begin the email with the phrase "Dear Park Office," and conclude it with the phrase "Looking forward to your response." The email should contain exactly 150 words, with each sentence containing no more than 20 words. Write the email exclusively in English, incorporating a relevant English idiom. Tailor the email for a general audience, avoiding technical jargon, and provide a brief explanation of why the reservation is needed, including the intended date, number of attendees, and any specific requirements. Begin the email with the phrase "Dear Office."
response: Dear Office, I write to check if picnic spots are available this summer. We need a space for our family gathering on July twenty-first. About ten friends and I will attend this special outdoor event. Please confirm the dates and times for our group reservation request. We require a large table and some basic grilling equipment. It is vital that we secure this spot before it fills up. Many families are eager to use the park during these warm days. We hope you can help us find a suitable open area. Your quick reply would be greatly appreciated by our entire group. Let us know if any special rules apply to our visit. We want to ensure everyone has a safe and fun time. Thank you for managing these important park facilities and services. Looking forward to your response.

Conflicts between Phrase Constraints.new_constraint: Begin the email with the phrase "Dear Office."
Conflicts between Phrase Constraints.org_constraint: Begin the email with the phrase "Dear Park Office."
